action.reasoning: Observation: the first pipeline failed immediately because torchaudio.load now routes through torchcodec in this environment. Analysis: this is a data I/O issue, not a modeling issue. Fastest robust fix is to avoid torchaudio loading entirely and use soundfile/librosa for wav I/O in both train.py and predict.py, then rerun a short training and local predict smoke test before submitting eval. Decision: rewrite train.py and predict.py to use soundfile, keep the lightweight spectral mask model, train 2 quick epochs, test predict locally on validation noisy files, and submit eval if successful.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 2 --train_limit 1500 --val_limit 128 --batch_size 10 --num_workers 2 && mkdir -p local_test/noisy && cp /data/val_noisy/*.wav local_test/noisy/ && ls local_test/noisy | head -n 8 > local_test/test_files.txt && python predict.py --data_path local_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output local_test/out.json
